description: Small format film, 35mm wide, also known as 135 format. Commonly used in compact and SLR cameras.
Medium format roll film, 6cm wide. Used in TLR, rangefinder, and SLR medium format cameras.
Similar to 120 but twice the length and without backing paper. Medium format, now discontinued.
Sheet film, also known as large format. Typically used in sizes like 4x5, 5x7, or 8x10 inches.
Subminiature cartridge format, 16mm wide. Popular in pocket cameras in the 1970s and 1980s.
Roll film between 35mm and 120 in size, 46mm wide. Used in vintage cameras like the Baby Rolleiflex.
Advanced Photo System. Compact 24mm-wide cartridge format, discontinued. Used in automatic point-and-shoot cameras.
Ultra-small format (8x11mm), used in spy cameras like Minox. Considered subminiature.
Disc-shaped film introduced by Kodak in the 1980s. Very low resolution, now obsolete.
